name: Black Pudding (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 8
(Always served with an Irish "fry". The preparation of this pudding may be impractical these days due to the difficulty of procuring fresh pig's blood and casings.)
1. Place liver in a large bowl and cover with boiling salted water. Cook until tender, about 30 minutes. Drain and discard cooking liquid.
2. Mince the liver and mix it with all other ingredients (except casings) in another bowl. Stir thoroughly until well combined.
3. Stuff the mixture into prepared beef casings using a cannon or sausage stuffer. Tie off the ends in one-foot loops.
4. Steam the filled casings for 4-5 hours, allowing them to cool completely before slicing.
5. Cut the cooled black pudding into half-inch slices and fry in hot fat on both sides until golden brown and crispy.
